about.title: About Me
about.paragraph1: Full-stack developer focused on fintech and AI-powered systems.
about.paragraph2: I design and build scalable applications with React, TypeScript, and Node.js, taking products from ideation to production. My focus is on applying artificial intelligence to create real competitive advantages.

jobs.title: Frontend Engineer
jobs.description: Working directly on the Iberia airline account, developing and maintaining large-scale, enterprise-grade web applications. I build new features, resolve complex issues, and optimize performance in mission-critical systems used by millions of users. Tech stack includes Angular 15 for modern development and AngularJS for legacy components and migrations.

jobs.title: Founder & Lead Full-Stack Developer
jobs.description: Founder and lead developer at Evolvio, a digital development agency specialized in full-stack projects with a strong focus on artificial intelligence. I lead the complete process: from understanding client problems, ideation, and solution design to full development, deployment, and optimization of web and mobile applications.

jobs.title: Frontend Developer
jobs.description: Development of full-stack applications with Next.js and React. Implementation of design systems and reusable components. API integration and performance optimization. Collaboration in software architecture and best practices.

items.title: Tesora
items.description: AI-powered financial management platform: connect your banks, unify your wealth, and receive intelligent advice.
items.content:
Tesora is an AI-powered personal finance platform that helps you control, understand, and grow your wealth more intelligently.

Securely connect banks, investments, and crypto, visualize your real net worth (assets minus debts), and receive personalized guidance from an AI assistant that truly knows your financial situation.

Main features:
- Secure account aggregation and unified wealth with history
- Smart expense analysis and automatic categorization
- Advanced debt management with Avalanche/Snowball simulations and personalized recommendations
- Marketplace of certified financial advisors with chat and granular permissions
- Conversational AI assistant with contextual access to your data
- Savings planning with realistic scenarios and projections
- Dynamic Financial Health Score and proactive alerts

With Tesora I've learned to see my money in a completely different way: I no longer just look at scattered balances, but understand how my income, expenses, debts, and investments relate in real time. I've discovered spending patterns I used to ignore, optimized my debt payments saving thousands of euros in interest, and started making much more conscious investment decisions thanks to the simulations and context the AI provides. Most importantly, I've gone from feeling lost with finances to having a real sense of control and clear direction toward my goals.
items.tagline: AI-powered personal finance: connect your banks, unify your wealth, get intelligent advice.
items.thesis: One place to see, understand and move your money.
items.thesisNote: One app — accounts, debts, investments, and an assistant that understands the full context.
items.problem:
Existing personal-finance products tend to live at two extremes: cold aggregators that only display numbers, or generic coaching apps with no real access to your situation.

Tesora was born to close that gap — a serious aggregator with an AI assistant that actually knows your wealth, your debts, and your investments, and can reason over them in real time.

stack.name: Next.js 16
stack.note: App Router + RSC + Server Actions. The layer holding up the whole frontend and half of the backend.

stack.name: React 19
stack.note: useOptimistic and transitions so financial mutations feel instant without lying to the user.

stack.name: TypeScript
stack.note: Strict mode across the board, input validation with Zod before anything hits the DB.

stack.name: PostgreSQL
stack.note: Supabase + Drizzle. Real Row Level Security — each user only sees their own rows, including from the AI.

stack.name: OpenAI
stack.note: GPT with tool-calling over a read-only view of net worth. The model reasons, it never touches credentials.

stack.name: Stripe
stack.note: Subscriptions, billing for the advisor marketplace, and Connect to split payments.

stack.name: Tailwind v4
stack.note: Design system with semantic tokens; zero loose CSS inside components.

stack.name: Vercel
stack.note: Continuous deploy + Edge runtime for low latencies across LATAM and Europe.

features: Secure account aggregation and unified net worth, with monthly history.
Smart expense analysis and automatic categorization.
Debt management with Avalanche and Snowball simulations.
Marketplace of certified financial advisors, with integrated billing.
Conversational AI assistant with contextual access to your data.
Savings planning with realistic scenarios and projections.
Dynamic Financial Health Score and proactive email alerts.
items.architecture: I keep things reasonably flat: a Next.js frontend calling server actions and an Edge worker that orchestrates the assistant. The AI layer has read-only access to a view of the user's data — never to banking credentials.
items.learnings: With Tesora I've learned to see my money in a completely different way: no longer just scattered balances, but how income, expenses, debts and investments relate in real time. And technically, I've internalized that useful AI isn't the biggest model — it's the one with the right context.

items.title: Cosmo AI
items.description: AI productivity assistant: centralize your tasks, projects, and apps in a single personalized chat.
items.content:
Cosmo is a JARVIS-like AI productivity assistant that centralizes your tasks, projects, clients, and notes in a single web tool, currently in beta and 100% free.

Think of Cosmo as your personal mini ERP: a place where you can talk naturally with your AI assistant and, at the same time, manage your entire workflow without switching apps.

Main features:
- Intelligent conversational chat (talk to Cosmo like a teammate)
- Task management with priorities, dates, reminders, and visual tracking
- Centralized projects: all related information, tasks, notes, and clients in one place
- Personal mini ERP: integrated management of clients, projects, and notes with shared context
- Persistent memory: remembers your preferences, previous decisions, and work style
- Internet search when updated information is needed
- Integrations with your usual tools to maintain your workflow
- Experience (Exp) system and gamified statistics to track your progress
- Automatic weekly routine generator based on your priorities and goals

With this project I learned how valuable it is to connect an AI model in a real application and how it can solve different problems.
items.tagline: AI productivity assistant: centralize your tasks, projects and apps in a single personalized chat.
items.thesis: A single chat to talk, remember and manage all your work.
items.thesisNote: Your personal mini-ERP: tasks, projects, clients and notes living alongside an assistant that remembers.
items.problem:
Productivity tools are fragmented: tasks in one app, notes in another, clients in a third. Every context switch costs time and energy.

Cosmo unifies everything in one place where you talk naturally to your assistant and, at the same time, manage your workflow without switching apps.

stack.name: Next.js
stack.note: App Router and server actions for a personal mini-ERP that loads fast and feels native.

stack.name: React
stack.note: Conversational state and optimistic UI so chat and tasks respond instantly.

stack.name: TypeScript
stack.note: Strict typing: tasks, projects, clients and notes share the same contracts.

stack.name: TailwindCSS
stack.note: A minimal, consistent visual system across every view of the assistant.

stack.name: OpenAI
stack.note: Cosmo's brain: it chats, remembers context and runs actions over your workflow.

features: Smart conversational chat: you talk to Cosmo like a teammate.
Task management with priorities, dates, reminders and visual tracking.
Centralized projects: related tasks, notes and clients in one place.
Personal mini-ERP with shared context across clients, projects and notes.
Persistent memory: it remembers your preferences, decisions and way of working.
Internet search when it needs up-to-date information.
Experience system and gamified stats to see your progress.
Automatic weekly routine generator based on your priorities.
items.architecture: A Next.js frontend wired to a language model with persistent memory. The assistant accesses the context of your tasks, projects and notes, and can search the internet when it needs fresh data — all without taking you out of the chat.
items.learnings: With this project I learned how valuable it is to connect an AI model to a real application, and how that lets you solve very different problems from a single conversational interface.

items.title: BTC Tracker
items.description: Bitcoin purchase calculator and tracker to monitor average price, holdings, and real-time P&L.
items.content:
BTC Average Price is a web app designed to log and analyze Bitcoin buy/sell transactions with a focus on one key metric: your real average buy price in USD.

The app provides a clear overview of your position, shows unrealized profit/loss, and helps you make better decisions before selling.

Key features:
- Automatic average buy price calculation
- Real-time metrics: current value, BTC holdings, total investment, and unrealized P&L
- Live BTC price with periodic updates
- Full transaction history with edit, delete, and CSV export options
- Add transactions in 3 modes: manual entry, generic CSV upload, and exchange importer
- Dedicated importers for Binance, Coinbase, Kraken, and Revolut
- Automatic currency conversion to USD to normalize real cost basis
- Sell simulator to estimate proceeds, cost basis, and profit/loss before executing a trade
- Purchase chart to visualize entry evolution and average cost over time
- Market sentiment widget (Fear & Greed Index)
- Google authentication or guest mode, with user-based data persistence in Firebase
- PWA support to install the app on desktop or mobile devices

With this project, I worked on a real crypto personal finance use case, integrating market data, multi-currency normalization, and heterogeneous data imports into a simple end-user experience.
items.tagline: Bitcoin purchase calculator and tracker for average price, holdings and real-time P&L.
items.thesis: Your real average price and Bitcoin P&L, in real time.
items.thesisNote: Log every buy, normalize currencies to USD and know exactly what your position is worth.
items.problem:
Anyone stacking Bitcoin over time loses track of their real average price and returns. Spreadsheets don't normalize currencies or import exchange activity.

BTC Tracker focuses on one key metric —your real average buy price in USD— and combines it with live metrics to decide better before selling.

stack.name: Next.js
stack.note: App Router and data routes to serve live prices and support PWA.

stack.name: React
stack.note: Reactive state to instantly reflect holdings, P&L and sell simulations.

stack.name: TypeScript
stack.note: Typed transactions and currency conversions to avoid calculation errors.

stack.name: TailwindCSS
stack.note: A clean, data-dense interface that stays readable on desktop and mobile.

stack.name: Firebase
stack.note: Google auth and per-user persistence; guest mode to try it without signing up.

features: Automatic real average buy price calculation.
Real-time metrics: current value, BTC held, total invested and unrealized P&L.
Live BTC price with periodic refresh.
Full transaction history with editing, deletion and CSV export.
Add activity in 3 modes: manual, generic CSV and exchange importer.
Dedicated importer for Binance, Coinbase, Kraken and Revolut.
Automatic currency conversion to USD to normalize real cost.
Sell simulator to estimate proceeds, cost basis and profit before executing.
Market sentiment widget (Fear & Greed Index) and PWA support.
items.architecture: A Next.js app with PWA support, per-user data persisted in Firebase and a live BTC price. The exchange-specific importers normalize currencies to USD so the average cost reflects reality.
items.learnings: With this project I worked through a real personal-crypto-finance case: integrating market data, normalizing multiple currencies and importing heterogeneous sources, all inside a simple experience for the end user.

items.title: TalkBook
items.description: Personal reading library with chat about your books, built with React, Supabase and a mobile-first experience.
items.content:
TalkBook is a web application for readers who want to have their personal library always at hand and be able to talk about what they read.

You can save the books you're reading or have read, with their covers, authors, descriptions, and ISBN, and keep your progress organized in a single place. You can also search for new titles using the Google Books API and easily add books to your library.

The most special feature of TalkBook is the book chat: you can open a conversation centered on a specific title and talk about its content, key ideas, questions, or reflections, always keeping the context of that book.

Main features:
- Personal library of read and reading books
- Book cards with cover, author, description, and basic information
- Google Books search integration
- Ability to quickly add books to your collection
- Chat associated with each book to talk about what you're reading
- Mobile-first interface with bottom navigation and clean design

With this project I was able to explore how to design a richer reading digital experience, where not only do you save your books, but you can also talk about them and turn your library into an active reflection space.
items.tagline: Personal reading library with a chat about your books, built with React, Supabase and a mobile-first experience.
items.thesis: Your library — but built to talk to your books.
items.thesisNote: Save what you read and open a conversation focused on each title, without losing context.
items.problem:
We tend to save what we read in dead lists we never revisit. Reading is left without a space to reflect on its ideas.

TalkBook turns your library into an active place: you organize your books and, above all, talk to each one about its content, key ideas and your questions.

stack.name: React
stack.note: Components for book cards, search and per-title chat with clear state.

stack.name: TypeScript
stack.note: Typed models for books, progress and conversations.

stack.name: Vite
stack.note: A lightweight SPA with instant builds and reloads during development.

stack.name: TailwindCSS
stack.note: Clean, mobile-first design with bottom navigation.

stack.name: Supabase
stack.note: Auth, database and storage for the library; syncs your collection across devices.

features: Personal library of read and currently-reading books.
Book cards with cover, author, description and ISBN.
Title search connected to the Google Books API.
Quickly add books to your collection.
A chat tied to each book to talk about what you're reading.
Mobile-first interface with bottom navigation and a clean design.
items.architecture: A Vite + React SPA with Supabase as the backend (auth, data and storage) and Google Books API integration to discover and add titles. The design is mobile-first, with bottom navigation.
items.learnings: With this project I explored how to design a richer digital reading experience, where you don't just store your books but talk about them, turning the library into a space for active reflection.

items.title: Roundtable
items.description: AI-powered thinking tool: configure a personal roundtable of historical and modern thinkers to debate ideas and improve decision-making.
items.content:
Roundtable is a thinking and decision-making tool inspired by a practice described by Napoleon Hill: sitting with a personal council of trusted references to debate ideas and gain clarity.

The app allows you to create your own roundtable of historical and modern figures — philosophers, entrepreneurs, scientists, and leaders — each represented by an AI agent trained on their mental models and decision frameworks.

Through an orchestrated multi-agent debate, agents don't just respond to the user, they challenge each other, surface trade-offs, and expose blind spots.

Main features:
- Configurable roundtables with multiple AI agents
- Distinct mental models and perspectives per agent
- Orchestrated debates and moderated discussions
- Decision-focused conversations, not generic answers
- Designed for clarity, reflection, and better thinking
items.tagline: AI thinking tool: assemble a roundtable of historical and modern thinkers to debate ideas and decide better.
items.thesis: A council of brilliant minds debating your decision.
items.thesisNote: Agents that don't just answer you: they challenge each other and surface your blind spots.
items.problem:
Making good decisions alone is hard: we lack perspectives and fall into blind spots.

Inspired by a practice described by Napoleon Hill, Roundtable seats you with a personal council of thinkers —represented by AI agents— who debate your dilemma from different mental frameworks.

stack.name: Vite
stack.note: A fast SPA for a fluid, reload-free debate experience.

stack.name: React
stack.note: Orchestrates the multi-agent conversation and the live debate state.

stack.name: TypeScript
stack.note: Typed agents, turns and messages to keep the debate coherent.

stack.name: TailwindCSS
stack.note: An interface focused on reading the debate, with no visual noise.

stack.name: OpenAI
stack.note: Each thinker is an agent with its own mental models; I orchestrate a debate that challenges itself.

stack.name: PostHog
stack.note: Product analytics to understand how debates are used and improve the experience.

features: Configurable roundtables with multiple AI agents.
Different mental models and perspectives per agent.
Orchestrated debates and moderated discussions.
Decision-focused conversations, not generic answers.
Designed for clarity, reflection and better thinking.
items.architecture: A Vite + React SPA that orchestrates several OpenAI agents in a moderated debate; each agent reasons with its own mental models and reacts to the others. PostHog provides product analytics on real usage.
items.learnings: Building Roundtable taught me to design multi-agent systems where the value isn't a single answer but the contrast of perspectives. Orchestrating agents that challenge each other —and keeping the debate focused on the decision— is as much a design problem as a prompting one.

items.title: Evolvio Website
items.description: Website for the digital development agency Evolvio.
items.content:
Evolvio is a digital innovation agency that designs and develops custom solutions for SMEs and companies: websites that convert, AI automations, and online presence strategies that drive real growth (up to x10, according to their claim).

They specialize in transforming typical problems ('your website doesn't sell', time wasted on repetitive tasks, inefficient management) into competitive advantages through custom development and intelligent technology.

Main features:
- Modern web development: responsive sites, optimized for conversion (UX, powerful CTAs, on-page SEO, behavior analysis)
- AI automations and integrations: smart integrations to save hours and improve customer experience
- Online presence and digital marketing (coming soon expanded): visibility, qualified traffic, and digital branding
- Professional websites from €500 (offer): responsive, basic SEO, hosting + SSL 1 year, 24/7 support and high availability
- Clear iterative process: Discovery → Strategy → Implementation → Continuous optimization
- Focus on measurable results: more visits, more sales, 99.99% online, secure and fast solutions
- Real social proof: testimonials and case studies from clients like Suicalsa, Tierra de Fuego, RCI, or Motoreciclajesp

With Evolvio I've learned to see digital presence not as an expense, but as a direct growth lever. Understanding that a well-made website (that actually sells and isn't just 'there') combined with smart automations can free up valuable time and multiply results without needing huge internal teams. It has changed my way of thinking about digital projects: less 'make it pretty' and more 'make it generate money and efficiency'.
items.tagline: Website for the digital development and innovation agency Evolvio.
items.thesis: A website that doesn't just inform: it converts.
items.thesisNote: The site itself is proof of the agency's work: fast, conversion-optimized and SEO-ready.
items.problem:
Many SMBs' websites are 'just there' but don't sell. Evolvio needed a site that communicated its offer —websites that convert and AI automations— and that was, in itself, proof of its work.

The goal: a fast, clear, results-oriented site that conveys authority and generates qualified leads.

stack.name: Next.js
stack.note: SEO-optimized rendering and fast load times on every page.

stack.name: TailwindCSS
stack.note: A consistent design system for a modern, responsive website.

stack.name: TypeScript
stack.note: Typed, maintainable components to iterate on the site safely.

features: Modern web development: responsive and conversion-optimized (UX, CTAs, on-page SEO).
An automation and AI section to save hours and improve the client experience.
Online presence and digital marketing: visibility, qualified traffic and branding.
A clear process: Discovery → Strategy → Implementation → Continuous optimization.
Focus on measurable results: more visits, more sales and high availability.
Real social proof with testimonials and client cases.
items.learnings: With Evolvio I learned to see digital presence not as a cost but as a growth lever. A well-built website —one that sells and isn't just 'there'— combined with smart automations frees up time and multiplies results. It changed how I think about digital projects: less 'make it pretty' and more 'make it generate money and efficiency'.

items.title: La Última Versión
items.description: Website and podcast by Hugo and Juanma on AI, finance, and the digital future. We explore the limits of innovation every week.
items.content:
La Última Versión is the podcast and site where we cross AI, finance, and the digital future without the fluff. Every week Hugo and Juanma push the edges of innovation with a straight tone: more audit of tomorrow than hype.

Our promise: unpack the real impact of artificial intelligence and modern finance on our present. No beating around the bush, no unnecessary hype—just the definitive version of the facts.

Featured episodes:
- #01 (Mar 26, 2026): Will we live forever?, the habits that make you rich, and whether getting rich is luck or skill. Longevity, tech, daily decisions that build (or destroy) wealth, and whether the future is genetics, discipline, or probabilities. Site: laultimaversion.com.
- #00 (Mar 19, 2026): Clever vs. intelligent?, standing out in the age of AI, and the decline of IQ. Introducing the show, intelligence as a commodity, the clever-vs-smart contrast, and the debate from the Flynn effect to smartphones and AI thinking for us.

What we talk about (each episode crosses at least two pillars):
- Artificial Intelligence: language models, autonomous agents, and everything AI is changing.
- Technology: hardware, software, and the dynamics of who controls the stack.
- Finance: startups, big tech, and market moves that redefine who wins and who loses.
- The future: where the world is headed, who decides, and why it should matter to you.

The hosts: Juanma (software and science: code, papers, architecture) and Hugo (business and strategy: entrepreneurship, productivity). Two profiles, one obsession: understand technology with judgment and tell it without smoke.

On the site you'll also find vertical clips (highlight moments) and links to listen on YouTube and Spotify, plus the community so you don't miss a single release.

With La Última Versión I'm bringing something to the portfolio that goes beyond code: editorial product, a clear message, and a brand aligned with how I think about the future of work, money, and AI.
items.tagline: Website and podcast by Hugo and Juanma about AI, finance and the digital future. We explore the limits of innovation every week.
items.thesis: AI, finance and the digital future. No hype.
items.thesisNote: More an audit of tomorrow than hype: every episode crosses at least two axes of the tech conversation.
items.problem:
There's too much noise and hype around AI and finance, and too little thoughtful conversation.

La Última Versión was born to cross AI, technology and money without the hype: a website and a podcast that explain the real impact of innovation on our present, with a direct tone.

stack.name: Next.js
stack.note: The podcast site with fast pages for episodes, clips and listening links.

stack.name: TailwindCSS
stack.note: The brand's visual identity, consistent and editorial across the site.

stack.name: TypeScript
stack.note: A typed, maintainable base to grow episode by episode.

features: Podcast website with each episode and its show notes.
Vertical clips featuring the highlights of every show.
Direct links to listen on YouTube and Spotify.
A community so you don't miss a single version.
Brand identity and message aligned with the future of work, money and AI.
items.learnings: With La Última Versión I bring something beyond code to my portfolio: an editorial product, a clear message and a brand aligned with how I think about the future of work, money and AI.

items.title: Zentunes
items.description: Web player of relaxing sounds for working, sleeping, or relaxing.
items.content:
ZenTunes is a high-quality relaxing sounds web player, designed to help you focus, relax, or sleep with ambiances like rain, forest, waves, or coffee shop.

It's a side project created by Hugo in 24 hours as a quick demo, designed to be simple, beautiful, and effective.

Main features:
- High-quality ambient sounds for background (rain, wind, nature, coffee, etc.)
- Mix multiple sounds simultaneously with individual volume control
- Background playback (you can continue browsing or working with the sound active)
- Minimalist and intuitive interface: choose a sound and start listening in seconds
- Light and fast: no installation required, works directly in the browser

With ZenTunes I've learned how valuable it is to have a simple, distraction-free tool to improve focus or disconnect. It has helped me create personalized sound environments that really work for me (for example, soft rain + coffee background) and I've discovered that sometimes fewer options and more simplicity is exactly what I need to focus or relax without complications.
items.tagline: Web player of relaxing sounds for working, sleeping or relaxing.
items.thesis: Fewer options, more focus. Ambient sound in seconds.
items.thesisNote: A side project built in 24 hours: simple, beautiful and effective, with nothing extra.
items.problem:
Ambient-sound apps tend to be overloaded with options and friction.

ZenTunes is the opposite: you pick a sound —or mix several, each with its own volume— and start focusing or relaxing in seconds, without installing anything.

stack.name: Vite
stack.note: An ultra-light SPA with instant startup, perfect for a 24-hour project.

stack.name: TailwindCSS
stack.note: A minimal interface that invites calm, with no distractions.

stack.name: TypeScript
stack.note: A small typed base to manage the mix and volumes safely.

features: High-quality ambient sounds: rain, wind, nature, coffee and more.
Mix multiple sounds simultaneously with individual volume control.
Background playback while you browse or work.
Minimal, intuitive interface: pick a sound and start in seconds.
Light and fast: no installation, runs straight in the browser.
items.learnings: With ZenTunes I learned how valuable a simple, distraction-free tool can be for focus. I discovered that sometimes fewer options and more simplicity is exactly what I need to concentrate or unwind.

items.institution: Comprehensive training in effective communication, sales techniques, and interpersonal relationship development.

items.degree: Master in AI and Big Data
items.institution: Master's degree in Artificial Intelligence and Big Data at the distance university of Madrid.
Higher Technician in multiplatform application development.
Technician in microcomputer systems and networks.
